steps.title: Identify the given equation
steps.content: We start with $$11m = 9(n + p)$$. To solve for $m$, we need to isolate it on one side.

steps.title: Divide both sides by 11
steps.content:
Divide both sides of the equation by 11:
$$\frac{11m}{11} = \frac{9(n + p)}{11}$$
$$m = \frac{9(n + p)}{11}$$
This matches choice A — A is correct.
